explicit_text: Machane Yehuda Market, or the Shuk in Jerusalem, is where I first went to buy baking supplies for a friend's birthday. It's a big market with many shops, and the particular baking supply shop I visited was located in front of Pizza Flora, which is now Machneyuda Restaurant. That shop had everything for baking, including fondant, which I had never used before. It was a memorable experience because it introduced me to the world of cake decorating and baking.
implicit_text: The bustling market in Jerusalem, often called the Shuk, is where I first went to buy baking supplies for a friend's birthday. It's a large market with many shops, and the particular baking supply shop I visited was located in front of a well-known pizzeria that later became a famous restaurant. That shop had everything for baking, including fondant, which I had never used before. It was a memorable experience because it introduced me to the world of cake decorating and baking.

explicit_text: Jerusalem is where all this baking adventure started for me. The Machane Yehuda Market there is a vibrant place full of shops, including the one where I bought my first fondant. I also made wedding cakes for friends who got married nearby, including one in Old Jaffa but with motifs from Jerusalem like the Dome of the Rock. Living and working in Jerusalem, I balanced my pharmacist job with my passion for baking, which grew over the years.

explicit_text: Pizza Flora was a landmark in Jerusalem near the baking supply shop I frequented in Machane Yehuda Market. The shop was located right in front of Pizza Flora, which was one of my favorite places before it closed. Later, the spot became home to Machneyuda Restaurant. Pizza Flora helped me orient myself in the market and is part of the backdrop of my early baking experiences.

explicit_text: Machneyuda is a well-known restaurant in Jerusalem that now occupies the space where the baking supply shop I used to visit once was. I remember that the shop was across from Pizza Flora, and Machneyuda took over that area with a few restaurants nearby. Although I never worked there, Machneyuda is part of the culinary scene that surrounds my baking journey in Jerusalem.

explicit_text: Fondant was a completely new ingredient for me when I first bought it at the baking supply shop in Machane Yehuda Market. It's a sugar paste used to decorate cakes, almost like edible clay, and I was fascinated by how creative you can get with it. Using fondant to decorate cupcakes for my friend's birthday sparked my passion for cake decorating, and it became a staple in my baking projects, including wedding cakes and birthday cakes for family.

explicit_text: Betty Crocker cake mix was my go-to when I first started baking cakes, especially for my nephew's third birthday about eleven years ago. I didn't know much about baking then, so using Betty Crocker was an easy way to make a chocolate cake that impressed my family. It was a staple in my house and helped me get started before I moved on to making cakes from scratch.

explicit_text: Making macarons was one of the challenges that really pushed my baking skills forward. After a couple of years of baking, I decided to try macarons, which are notoriously difficult to get right. It took me about ten to twelve tries before I made a good one, but once I succeeded, it motivated me to keep improving and trying new recipes like cheesecakes and more.

explicit_text: YouTube was a valuable resource for me when I first started decorating cakes with fondant. I watched videos to learn how to create cute figurines and decorations using sugar paste. It helped me gain confidence and creativity in cake decorating before I took formal pastry classes.

I watched videos to learn how to create cute figurines and decorations using that medium. It helped me gain confidence and creativity in cake decorating before I took formal pastry classes.

explicit_text: Old Jaffa is where some friends of mine got married, and I had the honor of making their wedding cake. They loved pistachios, so I made a three-tiered chocolate pistachio cake with silhouettes of them and places from Jerusalem like the Dome of the Rock. It was a special project that combined my baking skills with meaningful personal touches.
